question: Which statement best describes X-rays and their common medical use?

options.text: They are electromagnetic waves used to create internal body images.
options.explanation: X-rays are electromagnetic waves that produce images of internal body structures.

options.text: They are sound waves used to measure blood flow.
options.explanation: Sound waves describe ultrasound, not X-rays.

options.text: They are magnetic fields used to image soft tissues only.
options.explanation: Magnetic fields describe MRI, not X-ray imaging.

options.text: They are radioactive particles used only for cancer treatment.
options.explanation: X-rays are not radioactive particles and are used for diagnosis, not only treatment.

question: Which statement best describes a nuclear scan?

options.text: It uses radioactive material and a special camera to image body structure and function.
options.explanation: Nuclear scans involve a small amount of radioactive material detected by a special camera to assess structure and function.

options.text: It uses ultrasound waves to create images of soft tissues.
options.explanation: Ultrasound uses sound waves, not radioactive substances, so it is not a nuclear scan.

options.text: It uses magnetic fields and radio waves to image organs.
options.explanation: Magnetic fields and radio waves describe MRI, not nuclear scanning.

options.text: It uses X-rays only to show bone fractures.
options.explanation: X-rays are used in radiography, not the radioactive tracer technique of nuclear scans.
